intro: Connect a data source, then go AI designs → review & refine → save. Once saved it appears in your datasets. Structure analysis runs automatically in the background, so press "View structure" only when you want to check it.

restore.note: Restored your previous work (re-select the source only if you need to re-run).
restore.clearConfirm: This clears everything in progress, including the AI-proposed design and save results. Continue?

redesign.banner: Revising the design of the existing dataset {{name}}. Saving updates this same dataset's mapping (apply the data via "Update data (re-ingest)").
redesign.bannerAdopted: This design is saved as the dataset {{name}}. Further saves (redo / re-save after an AI fix) update this same dataset — no new dataset is created.

step.guard: Generate a schema first in "AI designs".

source.label: Data source
source.note: Any structured source planned (currently CSV/TSV/instrument text (.txt/.dat/.asc) / JSON / documents)
source.footSelected: {{n}} file(s) selected — the same source is used in every step
source.footEmpty: The source you choose here is shared across every step

design.hint: The AI proposes a draft of the data's blueprint (vocabulary) and ingestion rules. It uses the model selected in Settings.
design.hintsLegend: Hints (optional — checking the ones that apply improves accuracy)
design.domainFreePlaceholder: e.g. Seebeck = thermopower. Figures were read with WebPlotDigitizer.
design.inspectDetails: Show the structure analysis this proposal used

review.hint: Review the proposal; comment on what you want changed and the AI regenerates it (optional).
review.commentPlaceholder: e.g. Make the Sample IRI a composite key of (SID, sample_id). Update the ingester and design rationale to match.

save.hint: Splits the design into 4 artifacts and runs 8 automatic checks before saving to your datasets.
save.duplicateNote: Already saved, so the save button is disabled. Redoing via "Save again" below updates this same dataset — it never creates another one.
save.incompleteHeading: The design was saved, but it can't be ingested as-is
save.noRml: No conversion rule (RML) was generated, so this data can't be ingested. Redesign it, or fill it in via "Review & refine".
save.incomplete: The design is incomplete, so it can't be ingested as-is. Redesign it, or fill it in via "Review & refine".
save.validationHead: Validating the design against the source data found these problems (fix them here before ingest):

fix.hint: The validation above reported failures and/or warnings. In one click, hand those specifics to the AI to rebuild the design (including §RML). After it regenerates, press "Confirm & save" again to re-validate.
fix.needKey: To let the AI fix this, set up a model and key in Settings.
fix.commentIntro: Fix the following validation errors and rebuild §RML (use only the allowed Tier 0 functions; do not reference columns that don't exist):

autocorrect.converged: The AI auto-validated the design (resolved every issue in {{rounds}} self-correction round(s))
autocorrect.bestEffort: The AI self-corrected {{rounds}} round(s) but {{count}} issue(s) remain
autocorrect.cleanFirst: The AI's first design had no static issues
autocorrect.prefillFix: Copied the remaining issues into the fix box below — use “Ask AI to fix” to retry.
autocorrect.caveat: This is a static check against the real data; convergence does NOT guarantee it will ingest (the ingest gate re-checks).
autocorrect.tabularCaveat: References into JSON/XML sources were NOT column-checked.
autocorrect.coverageCaveat: The self-correction may have reduced how much of the source is mapped — review the design's coverage.

job.elapsed: {{mm}}m {{ss}}s elapsed · usually 1–6 min · connection is kept alive
job.elapsedStatus: {{mm}}m {{ss}}s elapsed · usually 1–6 min · connection is kept alive · status: {{status}}
job.resumedFailed: Could not resume the interrupted job: {{message}}

materialize.summaryOk: No blocking issues — OK to save
materialize.summaryBad: Blocking issues found (code {{code}}) — fix before saving

trap.T1: IRI uniqueness (is the composite key globally unique?)
trap.T2: BOM (does the ingester open with utf-8-sig?)
trap.T5: Mermaid colon avoidance (no : in diagram labels)

ingest.heading: Import data (human gate)
ingest.noRml: This design has no ingestion rules (RML), so it cannot be ingested. Once the "AI designs" step emits them, you can safely ingest from here.
ingest.note: When you approve, Morph-KGC runs this declarative RML (no generated code runs — only the verified Tier 0 functions) and ingests the result into an isolated draft graph. It does not touch Ask's default citation surface (canonical).
ingest.doneCount: ✓ Ingested into the draft graph ({{n}} triples).
ingest.notSaved: This design has not been saved yet (no dataset found).
ingest.needCsv: Ingestion needs the source file (CSV/TSV/instrument text) used in the design. Re-select it in the data source panel (re-selection is required after a reload).
ingest.cancelled: Ingest cancelled. Any partially written data has been discarded.

hints.compositeKey: ID columns may repeat across files/papers (a composite key may be needed)
hints.units: Normalize units and quantities (unify synonyms, etc.)
hints.jsonColumns: Some columns contain JSON (arrays or objects) inside cells

skeleton.gateHint: Check and fix each entity's subject key (IRI template) and classes. Properties are generated after this. A non-unique key collapses rows onto one IRI.

dialect.title: Read settings (optional)
dialect.hint: The read format (encoding, delimiter, lines before the header) was auto-detected. Correct it here before generating if it is wrong. Clean CSVs are not shown.

preamble.hint: Ingest the preamble metadata as columns broadcast onto every data row (so sample name, space group, etc. can be queried alongside the measurements).
